Description: General Notes for all Excavation Items: Contractor shall take all required permissions from the appropriate  authorities for Excavation, Blasting, Carting away materials etc. and shall bear all the expenses for the same.

Rate shall include 
1. Removal of all kinds of vegetation, small shrubs etc. If there are any shrubs, bushes, roots of the trees to be removed for min depth 1.5m including all necessary permissions. Lift & lead outside campus are to be considered to suit the local regulations.  The necessary approval shall be taken for removal of trees with the consent of the clients if applicable. The contractor shall be fully responsible for liasoning with local authoritites, compliance to all the statutory obligations, including all labour lanes in force, facilities, policies etc. towards all employees , labour and under no circumstance nor any acting party pass on the responsiblitites of the contractor  to the other contractor/clients.

2. Contractor shall get acquainted with site conditions, nature of soil and adopt suitable dewatering system as deemed fit for the nature of soil and prevailing water table and water collected by runoff or rain needs to be disposed off from the site.  The rate shall include keeping the excavated surface dry till completion of the scope of work. Dewatered water/ Slush  shall be drained out by suitable means to places not objected by civic authorities. The contractor shall submit the method statement for dewatering and the same shall be let out of the building premises through proper pipes & channels as required.
3. The rate shall include necessary general shoring, strutting with approved timber/steel accessories wherever necessary as approved by the Client / the Engineer.

4. Contour level to be taken jointly with the Project Manager and prior written approval be taken before starting any excavation/ filling at every 5 mt grids for arriving at the actual quantity using simpsons rule.  Cost to include all and necessary royalty and other permission charges required to execute the works.
5. The rate shall include all necessary setting out of building, excavation line, barrication by grid/plot marking, survey by Total station, transfer of bench mark, etc., complete

6.The quoted rates includes seggregating of good quality excavated earth from the clay / silty soil and storing in designted area for further treatment within the site premises for future landscape purposes including preserving top 300 mm layer of existing soil to meet MOEF requirements.. No extra is payable towards double handling.

7. Quoted rates shall incldue the preparation of temporary access road / Pathways, cross over, foot path, tracks, hard standings, temporary ramp to approach the excavated level etc, and temporary drains, rerouting of existing roads, supporting systems for existing MEP services if any and re-routing if required, etc. complete. at no extra cost.

8.  Carting away all types of excavated earth outside or within the site shall be included and environmental norms to be followed for discarding excavated material with submission of document of proof.
9. Soil shall be classified as three types for billing purpose.

a.Classification under hard rock  excavation item which requires chiselling with compressed air/mechanical equipment's,controlled blasting, chemical splitting, diamond cutting etc. with necessary mechanical equipment's and loose boulders which requires splitting and handling.

b. Classification under soft/medium/ weathered rock  excavation item which is non poclainable, can be excavated using rock breaker attachments to JCB/Poclain, manual chiselling, etc. and loose boulders removal without spliting etc.
c. Classification under soil  excavation which is poclainable, can be excavated using JCB/Poclain etc.

10. Please note that the water and power required for construction shall be arranged by contractor and shall be included in the rates quoted. Necessary bore wells , bringing water from tankers from outside, DG, temporary power, power distribution lines etc are all part of the rates quoted.

11. The method statement shall include all safety features and protection to workmen, third party as well as property and services nearby. Methods of shoring and strutting should be fully detailed in the method statement. Removal of excavated material from excavated area, method of handling, method of storage (including any handling if necessary) as well as disposal shall be explained fully. Where controlled blasting is restricted, the contractor should ensure that all necessary statutory approvals are obtained in advance and kept in current and all necessary insurance and other covers shall be taken and kept valid in the case of sub-contracting this job, such cover shall include sub-contractor's workman also. Where controlled blasting is resorted to, contractor should take all necessary precaution for ensuring safety of life end property in the vicinity.

12. Carting away of excavated soil/surplus /dense soil/ disintegrated/ weathered/ soft rock /other materials away from the site to a place designated and not objected by civic authorities by mechanical means including lead, lift, loading, unloading etc., complete. Including taking necessary approval from local authorities, environmental norms to be followed for discarding earth with submission of document of proof and fee if any. For the purpose of payment, quantities from the excavated area based on contour profile shall be accounted and loose earth or truck load will not be measured and paid. Rate to include all royalties, Seignorage, taking statutory approvals, traffic permits from Police and Local body, as necessary to carry out the works etc., complete. Disposal should also to an approved site only.  No Lift / lead distance or limitation is specified or accepted. Contractor are required to examine the scope of work, inspect the site and find out before quoting the approved disposal points. No increase in rate is allowable on account of difference between lift / lead distances.

13. The rates quoted shall include for Excavation either straight, inclined or curved in basements, etc., including marking out, cutting and dressing sides and bottoms to true dimensions and deposition of excavated material as described.

14. Qty. shall be arrived at after taking block levels taken prior to and after excavation. The below mentioned quantities are based on soil report and the quantities may vary as per site condition. Quantity  mentioned is tentative and may vary to any extent depending upon underlying ground profile to be exposed during excavation.
15.Trimming all sides as instructed at site, levelling all bottoms, clearing out all loose earth etc. The rates to include necessary cutting, trimming and filling with compaction .
General / Mass Earth work Excavation - Material stacking within the site boundary

General Excavation in all types of soil including laterite reddish brown/Laterite reddish brown with patches/ dense soil, disintegrated/Rock boulders/ weathered/ soft rock etc (Poclainable)  by mechanical means (using JCB, poclain etc.,) and or manual, including levelling dressing / trimming the edges, with all lead and lifts. Rate to include stacking & spreading the selected excavated material away from the excavation area in layers within the site wherever specified inside site boundary wall as instructed by site in charge. and also include for removal of slurry generated while excavation and keeping the area free of water, etc,. excavating loose pockets and Rate shall be including all necessary Shoring, strutting, underpinning soil, adequate protection to existing adjacent building super structures etc.
Note: PCC area shall be considered for measurement (Working space & provision of slopes if required for execution shall not be paid separately).

Supply and filling in foundations and the area whereever specified with approved good quality filling materials in plinths, area development etc. wherever specified  in layers of not exceeding 150 mm thick including breaking clods,storing,transportation, double handling, watering, compacting each layer with vibratory compactor and at unaccessible places with wooden/steel rammers to achieve 90 to 95% proctor density at optimum moisture content, all leads and lifts, bailing/ pumping out of water to keep site dry while backfilling; cost shall include conveyance of all materials, labour, machinery etc. complete as directed.The rate to includes loading,unloading, hire and fuel charges for tools and plants and other incidental charges etc., complete. With excavated earth available at site

Carting away surplus earth/excavated material/debris/rock and like materials outside the plot and disposing off the same to the designated areas identified by the local authorities / project managers for dumping of such material, including spreading, compacting etc.,as per the norms by the local authorities.
Mode of measurement- where measurement is not possible by level.
i) Soil - truck measurement less 20% for voids.
ii) Hard Rock - truck measurement less 50% for voids
iii) Debris - truck measurement less 30% for voids.

Providing and drilling hole of 50mm dia for a depth of 750mm @ 900mm c/c spacing as shown in the drawing. Inserting re-bar of 25mm dia into these holes and grouting the spaces using cement,sand along with non shrink grout. Cost Including drilling the rock up to required depth below the PCC level with all leads and lifts, grouting, reinforcement steel, labour charges, aceessories, consumbles, cleaning the debris, Testing if required, all kind of materials, labours etc, curing, at all heights and depths, etc., complete as directed by EIC.

Providing Pre-constructional anti- termite treatment and creating a chemical barrier to the building by injecting chemical emulsion of required concentrations under grade slab and Grade beam,  around the Plinth, Plinth filling, trenches, junction of wall and floor, Retaining / side walls of basement floor, external perimeter of building, surrounding of pipes, expansion joints, below UG sump and etc.as per instructions of chemical manufacturer and all as directed.  The treatment shall be carried out strictly in accordance with the technical specification and conforming to IS 6313  or equivalent BS  specification.  The chemical to be used as insecticides for the treatment shall be 50% EC (Imidacloprid 30.5 SC) and the application shall be dilute one part of chemical 50% EC with 49 parts of water to get 1% emulsion and strictly in accordance with the manufacturer's specification.
Note : Measurement taken along external periphery of the building. Treatment shall extend to a width of 1m around the building. (Including 10 years warranty).

Adding of admixture to meet the required standards and specifications for various workability, consistency, self compaction, retardation, acceleration, surface finish, colour, uniformity, strength, shrinkage control, durability, fire rating, cohesiveness in the concrete as appropriate.

Limiting the temperature of concrete and heat of hydration by adopting various temperature control measures like usage ice water, soaking of aggregates in water, storage in shaded areas, transportation and pumping with protective covers etc to meet the requirements. Formwork shall be designed for close jointed rigid, durable and shall have adequate strength, water tightness and ensure easy removal etc., Props, shorings and bracings shall be all in steel. Shuttering & deshuttering sequence shall be 100%, 80% & 40%  from the slab to be cast respectively depending on the progress. The same is as per the details enumerated in General Notes. NOTE: 1.Specialized agency to be approved by Consultants/Client
1. The guarantee for waterproofing should be provided by the principal company for 10years for both material and application.
2.Guarantee/warranty from applicators will not be acceptable. An undertaking in this regard from the principal company should be enclosed along with the letter for approval of waterproofing agency.
3.Testing of the surfaces prior to start the work by ponding, grouting or sealing of joints, making watta at corners/ junctions etc are deemed to be included for all items while quoting rates. If any leakage is found subsequently, it has to be made water tight by the contractor free of cost. 
4.During construction, all the construction joints of water bodies should be provided with butyl rubber swellable bars at all construction joints, retaining walls joints both from horizontal and Vertical joints using standard manufacturers like BASF/SIKA/FOSROC or equivalent as approved by the Client / the Engineer.
5. It is the responsibility of the contractor to maintain safe and clean working conditions in all activities related to the water proofing works at all time.
6. Utmost care should be taken to prevent any accident during the waterproofing work as some works are carried out in confined areas and dealing with chemicals.
7. Those who are working should have personal protective equipments PPE's like hand gloves, goggles, shoe, Helmet, mask,etc.,
8.   Manufacturer's specifications should be strictly  followed to avoid any accidents due to improper handling of materials.
9. Materials should be stored as per manufacturer specifications.
10. Materials  should be stored with proper labelling to avoid improper usage of consumption of any material by any workers.
11. Material Safety Data Sheet (MSDS) should be available at the time of delivery  of materials.
12. Adequate  fire  protection  measures  should  be  available  at  storage  and application location as some of the products are combustible.

13. Risk assessment should be carried out before the work starts. Risk assessment should  contain all  possible  risks, risk  rating without  control  measures, control measures and risk rating after implementation of corrective measures. Tool box talks should be conducted  every week explaining possible risks and its control measures. .
14. Clean environment should be maintained all around the work place.
15. All vessels and tools used for mixing and application of chemicals should be cleaned using appropriate cleaning materials suggested by the manufacturers. 
16. Water and balance materials after cleaning should be disposed off in such a way that it is not polluting the environment
17. Approved  method statement and material details should be available before starting the work.
18. Waterproofing  work  should  be carried  out  on  concrete  surface  which  are minimum 28 days old.
19. Waterproofing shall be carried out on sound concrete surfaces only.
20. Manufacturer specification  for  handling, mixing, application and storage  of material should be strictly followed.
21. Water  pond  test  should  be  conducted  after  surface  preparation  and  completion of waterproofing work to ensure water tightness.
22. All materials used to be supported with third party certificates / external laboratory test report as and when insisted by the Client / the Engineer specifically while start of work or on need basis, the same cost is with the scope of concerned water proofing agency.
RETAINING WALLS - BASEMENT, STP, UG SUMP (EXTERNAL)

1. Surface preparation: Before starting of waterproofing works the surface of the wall should be made free of any loose particles, vegetation, grease or oily matters etc. All tie rod holes should be filled properly. All honeycombs should be repaired as per approved repair procedures. Treating the Construction Joints : Chase opening the Construction Joints by providing ''V " groove,  removing  the  dust  and  clean  the  designated  area following  by  providing with approved  nonshrink high strength cemenitious grout, finishing, curing etc. as per manufacturers specification and as directed. Any damp spots on the walls should be attended by grouting cement mixed with flow enhancers like Cebex-100 or equivalent until the leak or dampness is fully arrested. When PVC pipes are inserted in the designated openings in the slab, it should be applied with epoxy bonding agent sprayed with sand on the portion coming in contact with non shrink grout or mortar or concrete to enhance the bonding and packed with non shrink grout includiing scafolding as directed.  
All sharp edges and corners to be chamferred / haunched to receive the membrane.
2. Providing and laying minimum 1.5 mm thick self adhesive, self healing type SBS based membranes  minimum puncture resistance of 220N(ASTM E154), tensile resistance over 200%, Elongation over 200%(ASTM D-412) and tear resistance of more than 200N (ASTM D1004). The membrane shall be laid over a compatible primer as per Manufacturer's specification with sufficient overlaps.
3. This membrane should be protected with 250 GSM minimum non woven polyester mat for high puncture resistance laid over the membrane either loosely laid or fixed as per manufacturer specifications.
4. The SBS membrane as well as the protection layer should have sufficient overlap as per manufacturer specifications.
5. The applicator/manufacturer should submit the details of waterproofing near the penetrations on the wall.
6. The membrane should be terminated on all sides properly with watertight joints.

7.The manufacturer/applicator should submit the details and get client approval prior to start the work.
8.The contractor to include cost of termination of waterproofing system with necessary overlapping, joint treatment etc complete all as per manufacturer's recommendations.
9.Treatment shall be provided from standard manufacturers like BASF, SIKA, FOSROC or equivalent.

WATERPROOFING TREATMENT FOR UG SUMP,OHT,STP (INTERNAL SIDE APPLICATION)
Providing waterproofing by Surface method INTERNALLY by following methods with 10 years guarantee.

1.Treating the Construction Joints : Chase opening the Construction Joints by providing "V" groove,  removing  the  dust  and  clean  the  designated  area following  by  providing  chemical  injection  treatment  in the  form  of  pressure grouting by injecting cement slurry admixed with approved grout admixture,  in the  required  consistency  through  the  pre fixed  PVC nozzles, which shall include drilling of 18 mm dia holes, fixing of PVC nozzles with approved chemical' and final cutting the projected nozzles and sealing off the PVC nozzles after the injection  operation  is  over  with  approved  chemical,  nonshrink  rapid  setting grout, finishing, curing etc. as per manufacturers  specification and as directed.Tierod holes shall be filled with approved grout materials.
 2. Coving: Providing and Applying at the junction of slab and vertical offsets with cement Mortar 1:4 and approved admixture at a size of 75 mm x 75 mm.
3. Bore Packing:  Preparing the inside surface of core and PVC by roughening using suitable file to get better adhesion prior to packing works. Cleaning the surface by wire brushing followed by water jet to remove any laitance or loose flaky  particles.
4. Providing necessary formwork for packing the bore using suitable arrangement (depending  upon  site  conditions).  Applying  a  coat  of  epoxy bonding agent to enhance adhesion between  the packing material and other surfaces. Packing the gap using approved  non-shrink cement polymer based grout. Ponding tests for a period of 48 hours should be conducted before start to ensure water tightness.

5. Internal Side Waterproofing: Providing and applying two coats of acrylic based  polymer  modified  cementitious  waterproof  coating  over prepared surface with necessary primer coat as per the manufacturer recommendations. Removing all surface laitance and contamination free from loose aggregate or other sharp protrusions with fairly smooth finish by wire brush and grinding method and surface shall be damped with Water for application of  waterproof coating.
6.Waterproofing coating shall contain a liquid polymer component and a cementitious powder component which  shall  be  mixed  as  per  manufacturer's instructions and  applied over prepared concrete surface strictly maintaining the coverage & thickness of minimum 1.2 mm or as specified  by  the  manufacturer whichever is more . The  applied  material  of  Waterproofing  coating  should  have a Pull off adhesion of greater than 1N/mm2 as per ASTM-04541 and static crack accommodation  of  1mm according to ASTM-C836. Complete as per the Contractors methodology approved by consultants / clients.
Cost to include necessary grouting of porous areas, Haunching at the junction of floor and walls, termination at the ends etc, supply and spraying 2.36mm sieved sand on the coating while it is wet to aid bonding complete.
Ponding tests for a period of 48 hours should be conducted post protective layer to ensure water tightness.
Treatment  shall  be  provided  from  standard  manufacturers  like  BASF,  SIKA, FOSROC or equivalent.

Protective layer(Horizontal): Providing & Laying M15 grade screed concrete to required slope admix with integral waterproofing compound Conplast WL  of FOSROC make or Equivalent at the dosage of 125ml/bag of cement over waterproofing layer to a minimum thickness of 50mm or as per site requirement, complete. Treatment shall be provided from standard manufacturers like BASF, SIKA, FOSROC or equivalent.

Protective Layer (Vertical): Rendering plastering to internal wall surfaces by 12mm thk in CM 1:4 with waterproofing admixtures as per manufacturer's specifications and clients approval, curing etc. complete.Treatment shall be provided from standard manufacturers like BASF, SIKA, FOSROC or equivalent.

Providing waterproofing to the roof by the following method with 10 years guarantee, which consists of :

Single component pure polyurethane based  elastomeric seamless liquid applied membrane:
1.Before starting waterproofing  works the surface of  the slab should  be  made free of any loose particles, vegetation, grease or oily matters etc.treating all surface cracks, construction joints and honey combs by grouting with specialised nonshrink grout, injection grouting through nozzles, haunching the corners,bore packing and grouting the annular space between pipe and concrete as specified by approved Contractor and preparation of surface in all aspects to receive the waterproofing  Ponding test should be conducted for a period of 72 hours to ensure that there is no leakage from the slab. Any damp spots on  the slab should  be  attended  by grouting  cement  mixed  with  flow  enhancers  like Cebex-100 or equivalent until the leak or dampness is arrested. 
2.After treating  the  surface as mentioned  above apply liquid applied,  high performance, pure polyurethane based, single component,  low VOC, elastomeric, seamless, moisture curing, waterproof membrane, applied in 2 coats with roller / airless spray,(As required  by the manufacturer specifications but minimum thickness (DFT) shall pass 1.5 mm) with  over 400% elongation and sufficient puncture resistance and anti-root penetration capacity over one coat of compatible primer at a coverage as per the manufacturer's recommendation . The spray applied membrane should be seamless, free of any holes. Overlap   should   be   as   per manufacturer specifications.
3. When spray applied or brush coated procedure is adopted, the coverage of the material should be noted on a daily basis and to be submitted to client. 
4.At the expansion joints and termination, the detailing for the membrane application shall be submitted to the Client / the Engineer/QA approval prior to commencing the work.
5.The PU waterproofing membrane should be extended 300mm above FFL on the vertical surface and tucked into the wall by making a groove.
6.Ponding tests for 72 hours should be conducted after the completion of application of membrane to ensure water tightness.
7.Treatment shall be provided from standard manufacturers like FOSROC,BASF,SIKA / Equivalent.
8.Thereafter Providing and laying 250 GSM non woven polypropylene geotextile for horizontal surface.

9.Sand sprinkle to be given for vertical wall surface over the PU waterproof coating to make proper mechanical key for bonding the plaster surface using special bonding agents .

1. Before starting waterproofing works the surface of the wall and floor should be made free of any loose particles, vegetation, grease, oily matters etc. All tie rod

All Expansion joint treatment works shall be guaranteed for 10 years from the date of taking over certificate. This Guarantee shall outlive the contract and include that any defect noticed & intimated to the Contractor during the said Guarantee period shall be rectified  free of cost and in case of Contractor failing to do so within a reasonable time, the client / the Engineer shall reserve the right to get the same done at Contractors' risk & cost. Cost shall include reinstating /replacing other finishes in and around the expansion joint works.

The expansion joint systems executed by the contractor should ensure that the products and work executed shall be in compliance with standard norms, UV resistant wherever applicable, temperature variations, thermal stresses induced, anti-root resistance where ever applicable.

The system executed by the contractor shall withstand the building movements arise from thermal expansion and contraction, wind loading, drying shrinkage, creep, settlement, traffic loading, waterproofing  and fire proofing requirements till guarantee period.
Rate is inclusive of all tests to prove the soundness of the products and work executed meeting the codal compliances and above mentioned criteria.

The system shall be designed keeping the relevant code compliances, accessibility and international disability legislation, traffic types, traffic frequency, fire barrier, water tightness, building movements, settlement, construction practices, executed profiles, architectural intent and need, shape and size etc.
Contractor to adopt any other additional items and systems if required to fulfill the requirement of the performance of the system in the rates quoted to be included.

Cost to include cleaning the joint and making it free from moisture, dampness, oil stains, paint and any type of loose aggregates prior to installation of expansion joint system including necessary repair of edges/undulations with polymer modified mortar, micro concrete, self leveling course, curing coating,

Providing and fixing of expansion joints system designed with joint sealing system, Aluminum/S.S extrusions, Anchor bolts, sealants, tapes, capping’s, gaskets, etc., complete as per approved details/drawings. System shall capable of withstanding repeated extensions, compressions, vibrations and cyclic movement without loosing adhesion properties and resist deterioration by weathering, sunlight, ozone, water, salt water, oil, fuels, acids and most of the commonly expossed conditions etc., complete with minimum 50% movement capacity and capable of accommodating multidirectional seismic movement without stress to its components.

The cover plate shall be designed of width and thickness required to satisfy movement and loading requirements and secured to base members by utilizing manufacturer’s pre-engineered self- centering arrangement that freely rotates / moves in all directions.

The joints shall be either with extruded aluminum base members with self aligning and self centering arrangement support plates as per ASTM B221-02 or compression seal system with necessary pads. The system shall be such that it provides watertight barrior not only at expossed surface but also at the structural level and system shal be able to accomodate the required flooring thickness as appropriate. System shall be able to withstand varies traffic loads such as vehicales, trucks, forklifts, cherry pikers, trollys, fire trucks etc and various traffic frequencies as well as intensities.

The Self centering arrangement shall exhibit circular sphere ends that lock and slide inside the corresponding aluminum extrusion cavity to allow freedom of movement and flexure in all directions including vertical displacement.
Provision of Moisture Barrier Membrane in the Joint System to have water tight joint is mandatory requirement.

Cost to include cleaning the joint and making it free from moisture, dampness, oil stains, paint and any type of loose aggregates prior to installation of expansion joint system including necessary repair of edges/undulations with polymer modified mortar, micro concrete, self leveling course, curing coating,
